message:
refactor(config): simplify Load by initializing from defaults first

The config loading logic was duplicating default value logic: once in
Default() and again piecemeal after TOML unmarshaling. Each new config
section required adding default-setting code in both places, leading
to maintenance burden and subtle bugs when they diverged.

New approach:
1. Initialize cfg := Default() with all sensible defaults
2. Unmarshal TOML over the defaults (only overwriting set values)
3. Apply environment variable overrides (highest precedence)
4. Handle palette markdown precedence

This removes ~150 lines of redundant default-setting code and makes
adding new config sections trivial: just add defaults to Default().

Behavior is preserved:
- Unset TOML values keep their defaults
- TOML values override defaults
- Environment variables override TOML
- Palette markdown files override TOML palette entries

The only remaining explicit logic handles:
- NTM_PROJECTS_BASE environment override
- AGENT_MAIL_* environment overrides
- NTM_CASS_* environment overrides
- NTM_ACCOUNTS_* environment overrides
- NTM_GEMINI_* environment overrides
- Scanner environment overrides
- Palette file precedence (markdown > TOML > defaults)

This change also fixes a subtle bug where missing config file would
error instead of falling back to defaults gracefully.
